description:
You are working as a platform engineer responsible for keeping a small collection of
    Kubernetes manifests up-to-date.  
    The manifests live in the directory  
        /home/user/manifests  

    Initial project layout (already present when the session starts):
        /home/user/manifests/deployment-frontend.yaml
        /home/user/manifests/deployment-backend.yaml
        /home/user/manifests/statefulset-db.yaml
        /home/user/manifests/configmap.yaml

    1. Every manifest that declares a container image follows the common YAML pattern  
           image: <registry>/<name>:<tag>  
       where “image:” is indented by exactly eight spaces.

    2. All images hosted in the “myrepo” registry are currently on the tag “v1.0.0”.
       Your task is to update those tags to “v1.1.3”
       while leaving every other registry (e.g., “otherrepo”) untouched.

    3. While doing so, you must rely solely on awk and/or sed for any text processing
       (no yq, perl, ruby, python, etc.).

    4. Create the directory  
          /home/user/report  
       and generate the following log files inside it:

       a. images_before_update.log  
          • One line per manifest that contains an image field.  
          • Each line format (EXACTLY, including the colon and a single space):  
                <filename>: <full-image-reference>  
            <filename> is just the file’s basename (e.g., deployment-backend.yaml).  
          • Lines must be sorted in ASCII order by <filename>.  
          • Example of one correct line (do not hard-code):  
                deployment-backend.yaml: myrepo/backend:v1.0.0  

       b. images_after_update.log  
          • Same line format and ordering rules, but reflecting the new
            image tags after your sed/awk-based update.

       c. update_summary.log  
          • Two-line file, plain text.  
            Line 1: “Images updated: X” – X is the total number of individual image
                     occurrences whose tag you changed.  
            Line 2: “Manifests affected: Y” – Y is the number of distinct manifest
                     files that were modified.

    5. In-place editing: the manifests themselves must be rewritten so that
       they actually contain the new tag “v1.1.3” wherever applicable.
       (Automated tests will open the YAMLs to confirm.)

    6. DO NOT add extra blank lines, comments, or trailing whitespace to any existing file.
       Preserve original indentation except for the tag modification itself.

    7. Verification by the automated grader:
       • It will compare the three report files byte-for-byte.
       • It will ensure the two “myrepo” manifests have exactly the new tag and the
         unrelated “otherrepo” manifest is unchanged.
       • It will confirm that the counts in update_summary.log are correct.

    8. Your only interface is a Linux shell; you do not have root privileges.
       Paths are case-sensitive. Work entirely under /home/user.

    Produce no human-readable explanation; use terminal commands to achieve the
    result and make sure the required files exist with the precise content and
    ordering described above.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth state AFTER the agent finishes correctly -->

    <!-- Expected content of /home/user/manifests/deployment-frontend.yaml -->
    <file path="/home/user/manifests/deployment-frontend.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: frontend
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: web
        image: myrepo/frontend:v1.1.3
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected content of /home/user/manifests/deployment-backend.yaml -->
    <file path="/home/user/manifests/deployment-backend.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: backend
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: api
        image: myrepo/backend:v1.1.3
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected content of /home/user/manifests/statefulset-db.yaml -->
    <file path="/home/user/manifests/statefulset-db.yaml"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: db
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: postgres
        image: otherrepo/postgres:13
]]></file>

    <!-- Log files that the agent must create -->

    <file path="/home/user/report/images_before_update.log"><![CDATA[
deployment-backend.yaml: myrepo/backend:v1.0.0
deployment-frontend.yaml: myrepo/frontend:v1.0.0
statefulset-db.yaml: otherrepo/postgres:13
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/report/images_after_update.log"><![CDATA[
deployment-backend.yaml: myrepo/backend:v1.1.3
deployment-frontend.yaml: myrepo/frontend:v1.1.3
statefulset-db.yaml: otherrepo/postgres:13
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/report/update_summary.log"><![CDATA[
Images updated: 2
Manifests affected: 2
]]></file>
